descriptions: Garfield Place Boys was a constituent gang that made up the original South Brooklyn Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of the gangs that made up the original South Brooklyn Boys were the South Brooklyn Devils, the Garfield Place Boys, the SB Angels, SB Diapers, the Wanderers, the Degraw St boys, the Sackett St Boys, the Butler Gents, the Gowanus Boys, the Kane St. Midgets, The Little Gents, and the Young Savages.

descriptions: Peace Companies were formed from the Mahdi Army.

descriptions: The Popeyes enforced a policy against red-and-white color schemes on other clubs, including Escape Hell MC.
edges.evidence_quote: On multiple occasions, they would forcibly tear the patches off of kuttes of said motorcyclists. Some motorcycle clubs that would be affected by the Popeyes' rules included the Zombi MC, Black Angels MC and Escape Hell MC.

descriptions: The Tenth Avenue Gang merged with the Hell's Kitchen Gang in the early 1870s.
edges.evidence_quote: by the early 1870s the gang was forced to merge with the Hell's Kitchen Gang

descriptions: M27 is composed of M23 defectors, indicating a splinter relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, Bertrand Bisimwa emerged as one of the leaders of the new rebel group called M27, which is composed of members of the National Congress for the Defence of the People (CNDP) and M23 defectors.

descriptions: Fukushima Rengo is listed as an ally of Kodo-kai in the infobox.

descriptions: The Madonia clan (Resuttana family) exchanged information with the Di Trapani clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Nevertheless, they have continued to run the Madonia clan, issuing orders via Di Trapani and exchanging information with the Di Trapani clan, according to investigators.

descriptions: Hezbollah was a rival to Amal Movement.
edges.evidence_quote: Hezbollah emerged in South Lebanon during a consolidation of Shia militias as a rival to the older Amal Movement.

descriptions: Hezbollah and Amal Movement share a parliamentary bloc and jointly control government portfolios.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hizbullah and Amal Bloc controls together six portfolios in the government.

descriptions: IS lists Amal Movement as a non-state opponent, indicating conflict.

Dead Man Incorporated.description: Dead Man Incorporated (DMI) is a predominantly white organized crime enterprise founded in the late 1990s in the Maryland Department of Corrections by Perry Roark, James Sweeney, and Brian Jordan. Initially allied with the Black Guerrilla Family, the gang expanded into drug trafficking, murder, and other crimes. It operates in prisons and streets across Maryland, Virginia, and West Virginia, with an estimated 370 members. Internal divisions have emerged over ideology and direction.

descriptions: Dead Man Incorporated listed as rival in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black Guerrilla Family, Dead Man Incorporated, Black P. Stones, Bloods, Crips, D.C. Blacks, Nuestra Familia

descriptions: Dead Man Incorporated is listed as a current ally of BGF in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Current: , Black Disciples , Bloods , Crips , Dead Man Incorporated , El Rukn , KUMI 415 , Norteños , Nuestra Familia

descriptions: DMI was founded with permission from BGF and initially worked for BGF.
edges.evidence_quote: Roark was a close associate of the Black Guerrilla Family and received permission from them to start an organization to unite white inmates in the system.

descriptions: DMI initially worked for BGF, indicating a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Initially, DMI worked for the BGF, but soon began offering those services to other gangs by targeting rivals and correctional staff.

descriptions: Infobox lists BGF as an ally of DMI.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Black Guerrilla Family

Eastman Gang.description: The Eastman Gang was a predominantly Jewish-American street gang that dominated parts of the New York City underworld from the late 1890s to the early 1910s. Founded by Monk Eastman, the gang operated in Manhattan's Lower East Side, engaging in armed robbery, theft, illegal gambling, extortion, prostitution, and opium peddling. It succeeded the Whyos as a prominent non-Irish gang and had strong political connections through Tammany Hall. The gang dissolved in the early 1910s.

descriptions: Batavia Street Gang was affiliated with Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Affiliated with the Eastman Gang during the turn of the 20th century

descriptions: The Crazy Butch Gang was allied with the Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was allied to the Eastman Gang who were at almost constant war with the Five Points and Humpty Jackson Gangs between the late 1890s and early 1900s as they were incorporated into the Squab Wheelmen , an ally of Monk Eastman's organization.

descriptions: Infobox lists Batavia Street Gang as an ally of Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batavia Street Gang, Lenox Avenue Gang

descriptions: The Eastman Gang was at war with the Five Points Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was allied to the Eastman Gang who were at almost constant war with the Five Points and Humpty Jackson Gangs between the late 1890s and early 1900s

descriptions: Eastman Gang and Five Points Gang were rivals warring over territory and political work.
edges.evidence_quote: Eventually, the gang became involved in rivalries with other local gangs such as the Yakey Yakes and the Five Points Gang, warring over both territory and work as political sluggers for Tammany Hall.

descriptions: The Eastman Gang competed with the Five Points Gang for control of New York's underworld.
edges.evidence_quote: Monk Eastman operated a powerful Jewish gang known as the Eastman Gang that competed with Italian and Irish gangs , notably Paul Kelly 's Five Points Gang , for control of New York City's underworld.

descriptions: The Eastman Gang was at war with the Humpty Jackson Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was allied to the Eastman Gang who were at almost constant war with the Five Points and Humpty Jackson Gangs between the late 1890s and early 1900s

descriptions: Infobox lists Lenox Avenue Gang as an ally of Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batavia Street Gang, Lenox Avenue Gang

descriptions: Eastman Gang may have developed from Short Tails, indicating a possible predecessor relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Another gang of the era, the Short Tails, had its headquarters in this same area, and the Eastman gang may have developed from that group.

descriptions: Infobox lists Whyos as a rival of Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Five Points Gang, Whyos, Yakey Yakes

descriptions: Eastman Gang and Yakey Yakes were rivals warring over territory and political work.
edges.evidence_quote: Eventually, the gang became involved in rivalries with other local gangs such as the Yakey Yakes and the Five Points Gang, warring over both territory and work as political sluggers for Tammany Hall.

descriptions: The two gangs fought over territory, engaging in open warfare.
edges.evidence_quote: The rivals disputed claims to a strip of territory of the Lower East Side of Manhattan.

descriptions: The Five Points Gang and Eastman Gang were rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: This was in contrast to many of his fellow Jewish East Siders who predominantly joined the Five Points Gang's rival Eastman Gang led by its namesake Monk Eastman.

descriptions: The Five Points Gang was in conflict with the Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Ellos estaban frecuentemente en conflicto con la Eastman Gang de la misma área.

descriptions: The Lenox Avenue Gang was allied with the Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Eastman Gang

descriptions: Fullerton Boys members were originally from SZA.
edges.evidence_quote: Most of its members belonged to the LVM gang, SZA gang (Sarzana) gang and some from the Wah Ching gang.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra clans Agate and Guttadauro allied with 'Ndrangheta clans Marando and Trimboli for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2003 con l'operazione Igres si scopre un'alleanza per il traffico internazionale di droga tra i Marando e i Trimboli di Platì e Volpiano, dall'altra i fratelli Agate di Mazara del Vallo e i Guttadauro di Bagheria.

Richardson Gang.description: The Richardson Gang was an English crime gang based in South London in the 1960s, known for extreme violence and torture. Founded by brothers Charlie and Eddie Richardson, they engaged in racketeering, narcotics, extortion, and other crimes. Their reputation for sadistic methods earned them the nickname 'Torture Gang'. The gang was eventually dismantled in the late 1960s.

descriptions: The Adams family allegedly ordered the shooting of a former enforcer of the Richardson Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The shooting of the then 68-year-old "Mad" Frankie Fraser , a former enforcer for The Richardson Gang , in July 1991 was said to have been ordered by the Adams family

descriptions: The two gangs were in a turf war, a conflict relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: The Richardson gang and the Kray twins were engaged in a turf war in the mid-to-late 1960s.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Kray Firm as rivals of the Richardson Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kray Firm

descriptions: The Richardson Gang were direct rivals to the Kray twins.
edges.evidence_quote: South London was controlled at that time by the Richardson Gang , who were seen as direct rivals to the Kray Twins.

descriptions: The Richardson Gang is described as a rival of the Krays' Firm.
edges.evidence_quote: At Shepton Mallet they met Charlie Richardson, another inmate, who later formed the Richardson Gang, a rival of the Krays’ Firm.

descriptions: Ronnie Kray killed a member of the Richardson Gang, indicating conflict between the two gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Ronnie shot and killed George Cornell, a member of the Richardson Gang, at the Blind Beggar pub in Whitechapel on 9 March 1966.

descriptions: George Cornell moved from the Watney Streeters to ally with the Richardson Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Cornell was originally a member of an East End gang called "The Watney Streeters" and later became involved with the Krays. However, he changed sides around 1964 and allied himself with the Richardsons.

descriptions: The Cutters had a strong rivalry with Seaburn Casuals.
edges.evidence_quote: claims that the Cutters had strong rivalries with hooligan firms of the two Manchester teams, Manchester United F.C. and Manchester City F.C., the Sunderland firm Seaburn Casuals, as well as with the Chelsea Headhunters, a firm associated with Chelsea F.C., and the Millwall Bushwackers, a firm associated with Millwall F.C.

descriptions: Sheng Ji was created by an associate of Wah Kee's founder, indicating a cooperative origin.
edges.evidence_quote: Guān Fúshèng (关福胜) created Shèng Jì (胜记) in Thailand

descriptions: Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan (main faction joined ISIL)

descriptions: Pro-IS factions of the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan are allies of ISIS–K.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Subgroups Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan (Pro-IS factions since 2015)

descriptions: ISIS–K formed an alliance with IMU.
edges.evidence_quote: the newly ordained ISIS–K forged significant alliances with other Sunni jihadist movements in the region to include the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan (IMU), Islamic Movement of Turkmenistan (IMT), Uyghur East Turkestan Islamic Party (ETIP), Jamaat Ansarullah, Harakat Islami Tajikistan, and the Islamic Jihad Renaissance Party of Tajikistan (IJRPT), Hezb-e Islami Gulbuddin, and Balochi militant groups including the Pakistani Jundullah movement and Iranian Jundullah movement

descriptions: IMU distanced itself from Taliban after a Taliban operation against it.
edges.evidence_quote: The Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan both distanced themself from the Taliban and ended their allegiance after the Taliban's Zabul operation against it in 2014.

descriptions: Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan (anti-ISIS faction) is a non-state ally of the Taliban.
edges.evidence_quote: Non-state allies ... Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan (anti-ISIS faction)

descriptions: The Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan supported the Taliban during the war.
edges.evidence_quote: During the war, the Taliban were supported by several militant outfits which include the Haqqani network, Al-Qaeda and the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan.

descriptions: IMU is listed as an ally of TTP in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Al-Qaeda IMU (pro Al-Qaeda factions only)

descriptions: TTP and IMU have a long history of collaboration, including joint operations.
edges.evidence_quote: The TTP and the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan (IMU) have a long history of collaboration.

descriptions: TTP and IMU jointly carried out the 2014 Jinnah International Airport attack.
edges.evidence_quote: The TTP claimed responsibility for the 2014 Jinnah International Airport attack, which was carried out jointly with the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan.

descriptions: Bonanno family (through Salvatore Bonanno) attacked Buccellato clan in revenge.
edges.evidence_quote: After the murders of Stefano and Giuseppe Bonanno, their younger brother, Salvatore, took revenge by killing members of the Buccellatos.

descriptions: Magaddino clan feuded with Buccellato clan.
edges.evidence_quote: During the 1900s, the two clans feuded with Felice Buccellato, the boss of the Buccellato clan.

descriptions: Minore clan formed a strong alliance with Buccellato clan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Minore clan went on to form a very strong alliance with the Buccellato Mafia clan

descriptions: Bronx '95 was given hangaround status by Bandidos as a precursor chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: Bronx '95 – a club founded in 1995 that was given hangaround status by the Bandidos and was intended to be the precursor to a Bandidos chapter in Trondheim

descriptions: North Side Gang had conflict with Ragen's Colts, O'Banion shot a member.
edges.evidence_quote: the North Side Gang also ran into trouble with other ethnic gangs; in 1921, O'Banion shot Ragen's Colts member Davy "Yiddles" Miller

Abergil crime family.description: Israeli organized crime syndicate founded by brothers Yaakov, Avi, and Itzhak Abergil in Ramat Gan, Israel, in the 1990s. Involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, extortion, and murder. Key leaders were arrested and extradited to the United States, with Itzhak Abergil receiving multiple life sentences in 2022.

descriptions: Infobox lists Alperon crime organization as rival of Abergil organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Alperon crime organization

descriptions: Infobox lists Mexican Mafia as rival of Abergil organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mexican Mafia

descriptions: Abergil organization cooperated with Vineland Boys for expansion in the US.
edges.evidence_quote: US federal prosecutors claim that when the Abergils wished to expand their mob syndicate in the US, they turned for help to an upstart Latino street gang known as the Vineland Boys.

descriptions: Abergil crime family hired Vineland Boyz to commit a murder.
edges.evidence_quote: he hired members of the Vineland Boyz street gang based in North Hollywood, to kill Sami Atias

descriptions: Vineland Boys allied with Abergil crime family for protection and cooperation in ecstasy trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The Vineland Boys have formed alliances with Armenian Power and the Abergil crime family. The gang allegedly first teamed up with Abergils in 2000, offering protection and cooperation to the Israeli mafia's ecstasy trafficking ring operating in their territory.

descriptions: A suspected affiliate of the Grasso clan was arrested for the murder of a Gioffrè member.
edges.evidence_quote: presunto affiliato dei Grasso

edges.evidence_quote: Bullshit MC formed during the late 1970s in Tårnby, Amager as the result of a merger between the Filthy Few and Nøragersmindebanden
